title: Losing Trust in Justice Dept., Judges Call Out Its Lawyers’ Behavior
summary: The federal courts have long assumed that the government’s lawyers are trustworthy. Now judges across the country are criticizing their lack of candor.

title: Hegseth Strikes Female and Black Navy Officers From Promotion List
summary: The defense secretary’s decision to block the officers’ promotions appears driven by his anti-diversity stance rather than based on merit.

title: Appeals Court Says 28 Transgender Troops Who Sued Can Remain in Military
summary: The ruling gives a temporary reprieve to a group of service members while their lawsuit proceeds. The plaintiffs plan to ask the court to extend the ruling to all transgender troops.

title: Oil Prices Jump as Middle East Tensions Build
summary: Some analysts said the main international oil price, which was up 6 percent on Monday, could climb much higher in the coming weeks if the Strait of Hormuz doesn’t reopen.

title: Anthropic Files to Go Public, Setting Stage for Huge I.P.O.
summary: The artificial intelligence company, which is racing OpenAI to the stock market, has seen explosive growth over the last year thanks largely to technology that can automatically write computer code.

title: California’s Public Universities Went All in on A.I. Now They’re Tearing Themselves Apart.
summary: California’s public universities spent $16.9 million on A.I. during a financial crisis, and the result has been chaos.

title: How Box Created 13 New Types of Jobs Because of A.I.
summary: Box, a Silicon Valley software maker, expects to have more employees, not fewer, as it hires A.I. architects, A.I. solutions managers and other new A.I.-related positions.

title: Is A.I. Replacing Tech Workers or Providing an Excuse for Job Cuts?
summary: Tech industry layoffs are accelerating, and executives have been quick to say it’s because their companies are doing more with artificial intelligence, even when there may be more to it.

title: Scott Pelley Accuses CBS News Boss of ‘Murdering’ ‘60 Minutes’
summary: In an explosive staff meeting, Mr. Pelley, a correspondent for the long-running Sunday news show, blasted Bari Weiss, the CBS editor in chief, and Nick Bilton, the show’s new executive producer.

title: The Return of Blaming and Shaming in Public Health
summary: For years, medicine has tried to eliminate stigma. Now, Robert F. Kennedy Jr. is bringing back the language of personal responsibility.

title: Mills Reminds Maine Voters She’s ‘Still on the Ballot’ for Senate Amid Platner Controversy
summary: A month after withdrawing from the state’s Senate race, Gov. Janet Mills suggested she remained an option after the likely Democratic nominee, Graham Platner, faced a new scandal.

title: Are Texans Ready for Talarico’s Kind of Christianity?
summary: Jim Rigby, a pastor who rarely uses the word “God,” is a key to understanding the Senate candidate trying to pull off something unusual in Texas.

title: They Voted for Trump. Here’s How They Feel About High Gas Prices.
summary: With the midterm elections five months away, many Americans who backed President Trump are wrestling with pain at the pump and the war in Iran.

title: Alberta’s Movement to Separate from Canada Gets Its Moment
summary: In October, Albertans will get to say if they want to stay in Canada, or hold a referendum to leave. Will it settle the matter, or deepen the rift?

title: Alberta Will Vote on a Possible Referendum to Separate From Canada. Here’s What to Know.
summary: Premier Danielle Smith announced that she would ask citizens to vote on whether they want to stay in Canada, or hold a referendum to secede.

title: Joana Avillez Revisits Her Waterfront Childhood
summary: Joana Avillez took six years to illustrate a new edition of Joseph Mitchell’s “The Bottom of the Harbor,” which captures the salty New York neighborhood of her youth.

title: Inside the NYC Primary Between Antonio Reynoso and Claire Valdez
summary: The Democratic battle over a House seat in New York City has reignited old tensions over gentrification, identity and who gets to define the left.

title: What to Watch For in the NY Primary: Key Races and Why They Matter
summary: Congressional primaries in Manhattan, Brooklyn and beyond could test the strength of Mayor Zohran Mamdani’s socialist movement and reverberate across the country.

title: Marilyn Monroe Fans Descended on Palm Springs For Her 100th Birthday
summary: To honor her centennial, over 1,000 Marilyn Monroe fans came together to dress as the screen legend in Palm Springs, where her Hollywood career began.

title: Marco Rubio: Florida Centrist, Tea Party Darling, MAGA Warrior, President?
summary: The secret to the secretary of state’s success.

title: Why This Summer Travel Season Will Be a Nightmare
summary: Air travel could be disrupted into next year because of high jet fuel prices.

title: Giant Stone Urns Hint at the Death Rites of a Lost People in Laos
summary: Thousands of mysterious containers lie scattered across northern Laos. These “death jars” may have provided a form of communal interment, archaeologists reported.
